priority_order: implement three cuBLAS SGEMMs using cuBLAS handle, with transpose flags for B (CUBLAS_OP_T) and beta=1 for final accumulation, and row-major U shape {d,16} with leading dimension d
llm_advice.rationale: The ATen baseline is correct but not faster due to overhead from multiple GEMM and addition launches. A pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM implementation eliminates ATen wrapper overhead, uses transpose flags to avoid explicit B.T materialization, and accumulates the low-rank term with beta=1, matching the row-major/column-major mapping described in the qyh-style approach. This should provide significant speedup for the given d range.
risk_controls: add CUDA error checking after each cuBLAS call
verify correctness for all d in [3584, 4096, 4608]
include fallback to ATen if cuBLAS handle initialization fails
stop_conditions: speedup > 1.1 relative to torch baseline on aggregate
max absolute error > 1e-5 for any d
time budget exhausted (e.g., more than 1 hour of cumulative compile+test)

notes: Use ATen/cuBLAS for the d x d GEMM.
Explore kernels that fuse Y += A @ T for rank 16.
